attributes.description: Alerts when a queue's visible message backlog stays above the configured depth across the evaluation window, indicating consumers are not keeping up with producers. Pair with the oldest-message-age alert/SLO, which captures processing lag directly.
attributes.name: [AWS SQS OTel] High backlog

investigation_guide.blob:
## High Backlog

A sustained high `ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible` means messages are accumulating because consumers cannot drain the queue fast enough. With `alertDelay.active: 3` the rule only fires after the backlog stays above the threshold for ~3 consecutive evaluations, so brief spikes that recover on their own are ignored.

### Triage Steps
1. Check whether `NumberOfMessagesSent` spiked (producer surge) or `NumberOfMessagesDeleted` dropped (consumer slowdown).
2. Review consumer auto-scaling — are workers scaling up with load?
3. Inspect `ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage` to assess timeliness impact — it is the headline lag signal and has its own alert/SLO.
4. Check for downstream dependency failures throttling consumer throughput.
5. Consider temporarily throttling producers if backlog threatens SLA.

### Customisation
- Tune `backlog > 1000` to set the visible-message depth that is unhealthy for your workload. A healthy queue with consumers keeping up stays low; pick a value above your normal steady-state peak.
- Tune `alertDelay.active` to require the backlog to persist for more or fewer evaluations before firing.
- Adjust the `QueueName NOT LIKE "*dlq*"` filter if your dead-letter naming convention differs (e.g. `*deadletter*`).

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.sqs.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/SQS"
    AND MetricName == "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible"
    AND stat == "Average"
    AND QueueName NOT LIKE "*dlq*"
| STATS backlog = AVG(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/SQS/ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible`)
    BY QueueName, cloud.region
// Tune: sustained visible backlog depth that indicates consumers are not keeping up
| WHERE backlog > 1000
